channel.title: News on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in News on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Senior Developer Triggers HR Review After Describing Variable Rename As “Great Replacement”
item.description:
SYDNEY — A senior software developer has triggered an internal HR review after informing his team during daily standup that he had completed “a great replacement” across a large system.

Ryan Hollis, 40, reportedly made the comment at 9:36am while summarising work completed on a legacy .NET service, where several misleading variables, method names and configuration keys had been renamed as part of a long-overdue refactor.

“Yesterday I finished the great replacement,” Hollis said, referring to the process of replacing dozens of badly named variables across the solution with clearer alternatives.

item.title: Keir Starmer Understood To Have Attended Exit Interview
item.description:
LONDON — Sir Keir Starmer is understood to have attended an exit interview with senior Labour figures this morning after confirming his intention to leave the role of Prime Minister.

According to Downing Street sources, Starmer arrived expecting a short discussion about handover arrangements, only to be shown into a small meeting room where Rachel Reeves, Shabana Mahmood and Yvette Cooper were seated behind a table with a red folder marked Leaver Feedback — Keir.

item.title: Chinese Navy Floats Giant Aerial Drone Carrier Over Sydney
item.description:
SYDNEY — A giant People’s Liberation Army Navy aerial drone carrier floated over Sydney Harbour this morning before leaving unchallenged.

The platform, identified by Chinese state media as the Tiān Jī-class aerial drone carrier — roughly translated as Heavenly Strike — entered Sydney airspace at approximately 8:17am.

The enormous jet-propelled structure was roughly the size of a suburban shopping centre and was visibly equipped with drone bays, missile pods, sensor arrays and several glowing red lights.

item.title: “Just Turn The Things Off”: Man Laments NASA Still Contacting Voyagers
item.description:
WOLLONGONG — Local man Tom Frampton has criticised NASA for continuing to communicate with the Voyager probes, arguing that after nearly fifty years of flight, the space agency should “just turn the cunts off” and stop pretending the spacecraft are still part of a meaningful operational strategy.

Frampton, 43, made the remarks after learning that engineers still exchange signals with the ageing probes as they drift through interstellar space, billions of kilometres from Earth and moving through what scientists describe as “one of the most scientifically significant regions ever reached by a human-made object”.

item.title: “Britain Is A Tolerant Country,” Says Councillor Who Forgot Intolerance Created The Empire
item.description:
LONDON — A local councillor has praised Britain as a “deeply tolerant country” during a community cohesion event, apparently forgetting that the British Empire was not assembled through mutual respect, patient dialogue, and a firm commitment to celebrating difference.

The remarks were made at a council forum on inclusion, belonging, and shared values, where attendees were reminded that Britain’s greatest strength has always been its tolerance, apart from the several centuries during which its greatest strength was gunboats, conquest, railways, hospitals, common law, naval supremacy, abolishing suttee, suppressing piracy, redrawing maps, and arriving in foreign ports with the relaxed confidence of a people who had already decided they were in charge.

item.title: Sydneysiders Campaign For Transport Industrial Action To Continue After Discovering Transport Still Runs But Opal Readers Are Off
item.description:
SYDNEY — Thousands of Sydneysiders have thrown their support behind ongoing Transport for NSW industrial action after discovering that many buses, trams and trains are still running, traffic remains broadly fucked, and the Opal readers are mysteriously switched off.

Commuters who had initially reacted to the disruption with frustration said their mood changed dramatically after boarding a bus, instinctively reaching for their Opal card, and being waved through by a driver radiating the calm authority of a man who no longer cares what happens to the revenue model.

item.title: American Conservative Stresses Americans Are Not “Pussies” Like Australians While Waiting For Government To Take His Guns Any Day Now
item.description:
OKLAHOMA CITY — A tough middle American conservative has reminded Australians and Britons that his people are not “pussies” who simply hand over their weapons, while sitting in a suburban kitchen waiting for the federal government to do the tyranny thing he has been warning about since 1997.

The man, 54-year-old Dale Harkness, made the remarks after sharing a satirical article mocking Britain’s inability to defend itself from knife attacks, before adding that “this is what happens when a people become weak, dependent, feminised, disarmed and Australian”.

item.title: James O’Brien Demands Caller Name A Member Of The Liberal Media Elite Who Would Call Him Racist, While Calling Him Racist
item.description:
LONDON — LBC presenter James O’Brien has reportedly challenged a pensioner to name a single member of the so-called “liberal media elite” who would call him a bigot or racist for objecting to the transformation of his neighbourhood, shortly before spending the next nine minutes strongly implying the caller was morally defective.

The exchange occurred after 74-year-old pensioner Barry Wilkes told O’Brien he felt unable to discuss rapid demographic change, housing pressure, crime, school overcrowding and the loss of social trust in his area without being dismissed by “the media, the liberal elite and people like you” as a bigot and racist.

item.title: Employers: Your Job Ad Euphemisms Are Being Found Out. Here Are Some New Ones.
item.description:
For years, employers have relied on a stable vocabulary of job ad euphemisms to describe roles that are under-scoped, underfunded, under-managed, overexposed, politically tangled, or quietly on fire.

Unfortunately, candidates have begun learning the language.

“Fast-paced environment” no longer sounds exciting. It sounds like unpaid panic.

“Comfortable with ambiguity” no longer sounds entrepreneurial. It sounds like nobody knows what the job is.

“Wear many hats” no longer sounds versatile. It sounds like one salary trying to impersonate a department.

item.title: Unvaccinated Cops Welcomed Back Years After Trauma Of The Century Memory-Holed
item.description:
SYDNEY — Police officers who refused the COVID vaccine have been warmly invited to return to the force years after being stood down, dismissed, shamed, isolated, treated as operational contaminants and informed that public service was impossible without total medical compliance.

The move comes after officials determined that enough time had passed for the entire episode to be discussed in the emotionally neutral language of “workforce recovery”, “re-engagement”, “lessons learned” and “moving forward”, rather than the more difficult language of “we bullied people during a panic and now need staff”.

item.title: Revealed: World Cup Humanity’s Current Dispute-Resolution Mechanism For Preventing Global Thermonuclear War
item.description:
ZURICH — The Daily Thrum can reveal that the FIFA World Cup is now functioning as humanity’s primary dispute-resolution mechanism for preventing cataclysmic planetary destruction, after diplomats quietly concluded that allowing nations to scream at referees every four years remains preferable to letting them express geopolitical frustration through missile doctrine.

The arrangement, described by one official as “Olympic truce energy for a species with big pointy things”, reportedly allows rival nations to redirect territorial grievances, historical resentments, religious tension, economic humiliation, wounded national pride and unresolved border disputes into 90 minutes of men falling over near a penalty area.

item.title: Content Creator Host Focuses On Immigration-Housing Link After Realising “No Shelter” Harder To Lifestyle-Hack Than Traffic Or Medicare Collapse
item.description:
SYDNEY — A YouTube content creator has explained his continued focus on the relationship between immigration and housing, rather than immigration and every other overloaded national system, after realising that shelter remains one of the few social goods Australians cannot simply avoid needing through better habits, lower expectations, or leaving the house less.

Commentator Julian May, host of the podcast Mayday, uploaded a 52-minute video explaining why it won’t be long until the Royal Botanic Gardens becomes “an informal campground”, marking what viewers described as a logical progression for a man who has already spent several years warning that roads, hospitals, schools, energy grids and customer service departments were all approaching terminal failure.

item.title: Mistreated, Neglected Cat Has Not Been Petted For Over Ten Minutes
item.description:
SYDNEY — According to sources, a local cat is being cruelly neglected by his human after she allegedly failed to pat him for more than ten consecutive minutes.

Tubby domestic shorthair Tom Frampton issued the statement from the armrest after owner Carla Pickering briefly stopped stroking him in order to answer an email, drink water, and perform several other non-cat-related activities described by Frampton as “deeply troubling and infuriating”.

Witnesses say Frampton initially attempted to resolve the matter through established diplomatic channels, including staring, shifting his weight, placing one paw on Pickering’s forearm, and slowly extending a single claw into her sleeve.

item.title: Tony Abbott Raises Serious Concerns About Immigration Despite Tragically Never Having Had Any Political Influence
item.description:
SYDNEY — Former prime minister Tony Abbott has raised serious concerns about immigration, social cohesion and national identity, despite the tragic historical fact that he has never held any meaningful political power and therefore could not possibly have influenced any of the settings now troubling him.

Appearing in an interview, Mr Abbott cautioned that “people need to join Team Australia rather than simply live in Hotel Australia,” a phrase he delivered with the grave disappointment of a man who had apparently only recently discovered that slogans do not integrate people.

item.title: Man Hears About Company Called Palantir And Starts Googling Which Cyberpunk Movie It’s From
item.description:
SYDNEY — A local man has reportedly begun searching online to determine which late-1990s cyberpunk film first introduced the corporation Palantir, after learning the company was, in fact, real and involved in government, military, intelligence, policing, health, logistics and artificial intelligence systems.

Martin Prebble, 42, said he first encountered the name while reading an article about public-sector data platforms, at which point he assumed Palantir was “one of those fictional megacorps that harvests consciousness in the third act”.

item.title: “By The Way, There’s A World Cup Underway,” Media Reports
item.description: SYDNEY — Australians across the nation have been informed that a major international sporting tournament known as the “World Cup” is currently underway, despite the public having spent the past six months knee-deep in news about the cost of living, NDIS blowouts, capital gain tax reforms, the Bondi Royal Commission, returning ISIS brides, supermarket pricing, social cohesion, housing collapse, youth crime, energy prices and every politician quietly asking large language models how to govern.

item.title: Albanese: “When We Get Elected We’ll Fix Everything”
item.description:
CANBERRA — Anthony Albanese has reminded voters that Labor remains fully committed to fixing everything once it gets elected and he becomes Prime Minister.

Speaking at a press conference about housing affordability, energy prices, crime, and the increasingly ambitious task of buying a lettuce without taking out a personal loan, Albanese said Australians should remain patient.

“We have a clear plan for government,” he said. “The important thing now is making sure we get the opportunity to implement it.”

item.title: Podcaster Furious After Followers Start Tagging Him With “Owning Nothing And Being Happy Doesn’t Sound That Bad, Really”
item.description:
LONDON — British podcaster Julian May has reportedly entered a state of ideological emergency after dozens of users began tagging him on X with the phrase, “Owning nothing and being happy doesn’t sound that bad, really.”

May, host of the popular doom podcast Mayday, has spent years warning viewers that global elites intend to strip ordinary people of property, autonomy, privacy, national identity and the spiritual inheritance of Western civilisation before instructing them to “own nothing and be happy”.

item.title: AI-Assisted Development: A Translation Cheatsheet for Non-Technical Personnel and Non-IC Engineers
item.description: The Daily Thrum conducted a deep dive into the emerging dialect of AI-era corporate communication and uncovered a remarkable phenomenon: many commonly used phrases now appear to have meanings quite different from their official definitions. The examples below were collected from meetings, pull requests, status updates, delivery reports, and post-incident reviews. While these translations should not be considered universally accurate, our investigation suggests they occur with sufficient frequency to warrant closer scrutiny by anyone responsible for approving budgets, signing off releases, or explaining outages to senior leadership.

item.title: Former Special Operator Who Neutralised Eight Home Invaders Convicted After Failing To Wait Until Shot Politely Enough
item.description:
BRISBANE — A former special operator has been convicted of second-degree murder after defending his family from eight armed home invaders in what prosecutors described as “an excessive and worryingly competent response to a challenging interpersonal incident”.

The court heard that Damien Redfern, 42, a former special forces soldier, defensive tactics instructor, private security consultant, emergency-response trainer, and the only man in southern Queensland known to have reverse-engineered a household mop into a non-lethal perimeter deterrent, responded to the break-in “too quickly, too decisively, and with insufficient visible helplessness”.

item.title: Premier Who Doesn’t Know What A Woman Is Lambasts Billboard Truck, Saying “It Attacks Women”
item.description:
MELBOURNE — Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan, who has previously struggled to define what a woman is, has condemned a billboard truck portraying her as a witch, warning that the image represents an attack on women, a category which sources confirmed had briefly become legally, politically and rhetorically available again.

The billboard, which reportedly drove around Melbourne as part of a political campaign, prompted the Premier to denounce sexism in public life and warn that women in leadership should not be subjected to degrading language, imagery, or old-fashioned misogynistic tropes unless those women first require a departmental definition, stakeholder process, inclusive terminology review, and several footnotes.

item.title: Sprint Goals Missed After Senior Developer Learns Getting Ahead Just Means Being Rewarded With More Sprint
item.description:
SYDNEY — A software team has missed its sprint goals after a senior developer reportedly learned that completing work ahead of schedule does not result in rest, recognition, or any meaningful improvement in the human condition, but merely causes more Jira to be dumped into the available space like wet cement into a crack.

Ryan Hollis, 40, is said to have reached the conclusion after the previous sprint, during which he finished two large backend dev tasks early and was immediately rewarded with another ticket, a “small defect”, an architectural clarification, and what Product described as “just a quick piece of follow-up work while we have capacity”.

item.title: Protesters In Southampton Use Institutional Sympathy Language, Surprised When Police Crack Their Heads Anyway
item.description:
SOUTHAMPTON — Police have reportedly proceeded to mass arrests and beatings after protesters outside Southampton Police Station deployed familiar anti-racist slogans.

The crowd gathered outside the station chanting “racist police off our streets” and “I can’t breathe”, in what witnesses described as a bold attempt to activate the moral force field normally generated when those phrases are used by university students, NGOs, activist collectives or people standing beside professionally printed placards.

item.title: Leaked Documents Reveal Victoria Initially Had Plans To Incinerate Unvaccinated Neighbourhoods With Superheated Plasma
item.description:
MELBOURNE — Leaked documents have revealed the Victorian government initially considered plans to incinerate unvaccinated neighbourhoods with superheated plasma during the pandemic, before officials reportedly decided the measure could be perceived as “slightly coercive” if announced before a press conference on community safety.

The draft proposal, titled Operation Final Dose: Targeted Thermal Confidence Measures For Persistent Non-Compliance Zones, outlined a phased strategy under which suburbs with insufficient vaccination uptake would receive escalating behavioural interventions, beginning with text-message reminders and ending with “high-temperature area normalisation”.

item.title: Cosmote To Launch In Australia, Rebrands “What’s Up” Prepaid Tier As “Scarnon, Cunts”
item.description:
ATHENS/SYDNEY — Greek telecommunications giant Cosmote has announced plans to launch in Australia, confirming its popular “What’s Up” prepaid mobile tier will be localised for the Australian market as “Scarnon, Cunts”.

The rebrand follows months of consumer research, cultural consultation, and one marketing executive spending 11 minutes on a Sydney beach before declaring the strategy “basically done”.

“We understood that Americanisms may work in countries where English is a second language,” said regional spokesperson Thomas Framptonidis. “But Australians require a more authentic greeting architecture. You cannot enter this market saying ‘what’s up’ like an American teen.”

item.title: UK: Pensioner Arrested For Saying Government Should Not Give Legal Exemptions To Religious Minorities Carrying Ceremonial Weapons After Man Uses Ceremonial Energised Plasma Rifle To Level Building
item.description:
LONDON — A pensioner has been arrested on suspicion of religious hatred after saying the government should not give legal exemptions to religious minorities carrying ceremonial weapons, days after a man legally carrying a ceremonial energised plasma rifle was convicted of using one to level a building, killing 12 people and injuring 28 others.

Harold Davis, 74, reportedly made the comment outside a newsagent while watching a televised debate in which politicians, police officials, community leaders and sensitivity advisers stressed that the incident should not be used to question the broader exemption framework.

item.title: Grand Plan Announced To Accommodate Population Growth By Building Second Sydney And Melbourne Above First Sydney And Melbourne
item.description:
CANBERRA — The federal government has announced an ambitious long-term infrastructure plan to accommodate Australia’s growing population by building additional elevated street-level platforms above Sydney and Melbourne, creating a second CBD and surrounding suburbs directly on top of the existing ones by 2075.

The proposal, described as a “vertical urban continuity framework”, would see major roads, train stations, apartment towers, shopping centres, childcare centres, cafes, bike lanes, vape shops and unresolved social tensions duplicated approximately 300 metres above current street level.

item.title: Project Manager Says Global Nuclear War Date Is Uncertain, But Delivery Still Required By Q3
item.description: SYDNEY — A project manager has reminded the delivery team that although Ukraine remains unresolved, the war on Iran has set the Middle East on fire, the Strait of Hormuz is closed, Taiwan is under growing Chinese pressure, the South China Sea remains one naval misunderstanding away from disaster and the Korean Peninsula continues to exist as a heavily armed unresolved sentence, the exact date of cataclysmic global thermonuclear war remains uncertain, and as such the organisation is still expecting the customer portal modernisation programme to be delivered by Q3.

item.title: Job-seeking Candidates Must Traverse Corridor While Being Attacked by Masked Martial Artists In Tech Company's Final Stage of Hiring Process
item.description:
A leading Australian technology company has finally revealed that the mysterious “final cultural and technical assessment” in its hiring process is, in fact, a 40-metre office corridor lined with masked martial artists who attack candidates with nunchaku.

The company, workflow optimisation platform Tyrus Synergy, confirmed the practice after several senior software engineers already tried to go through it and became hospitalised.

“It’s important to assess candidates holistically,” said Tyrus Synergy Head of People Experience, Marissa Klent, standing beside a bloodied whiteboard reading Agile Mindset = Survive. “Anyone can talk about distributed systems in an interview. But can they explain idempotency while evading hits in the ribs by a man in a balaclava? That’s what separates a senior engineer from someone who merely has twenty years of commercial experience.”

item.title: Australia First Country In Southern Hemisphere To Host 3km-Tall Cubical Data Centre
item.description:
NARRABRI — Australia has become the first country in the Southern Hemisphere to approve construction of a three-kilometre-tall cubical data centre, a structure expected to dominate the skyline and local weather systems.

The facility, known as TETRYAX-3000, will be built in regional New South Wales after similar structures were successfully deployed across North America, Western Europe, China, South Korea and Japan.

Government officials said the project would create thousands of jobs and position Australia at the forefront of the global artificial intelligence economy.

item.title: Vivid Drone Show Accidentally Summons Demonic Figure
item.description:
SYDNEY — Vivid Sydney organisers have apologised after a scheduled drone show over the harbour unexpectedly formed and animated a demonic figure, causing thousands of families to look up from Circular Quay and briefly wonder whether the city’s annual light festival had entered its final boss phase.

The incident occurred at 8:47pm during what was billed as Light, Country, Connection, a family-friendly aerial display celebrating creativity, community and “the shared luminous story of place”. Witnesses say the drones initially formed a whale, a banksia flower, the Sydney Opera House and several abstract shapes representing reconciliation, before abruptly rearranging into a colossal horned demonic body above the Harbour Bridge.

item.title: Victorian Government Confirms Machete Ban Remains Effective Against Citizens Already Unlikely To Bring A Machete To A Fight
item.description:
MELBOURNE — The Victorian Government has confirmed its machete ban remains highly effective against all citizens who were already unlikely to bring a machete to a fight, despite reports that more than 40 public knife fights have occurred in the year since the policy was announced.

Officials defended the ban after critics described it as an “absolute failure”, saying the policy had successfully prevented thousands of accountants, nurses, retirees, office workers, parents, librarians and people waiting quietly for trams from carrying machetes in public.

item.title: Trial of night-sky advertising system announced
item.description:
The government has confirmed Sydney and Melbourne will be among the first cities in the world to trial a low-earth orbit microsatellite network capable of displaying advertisements, public safety alerts, news headlines and sponsored civic messaging across the night sky.

The programme, known as SkyReach, will use thousands of synchronised microsatellites to form a “dynamic orbital visibility layer” above major population centres between sunset and 3am, a period officials described as “historically underutilised inventory”.

item.title: Software developer’s daily stand-up update was “same shit as yesterday”
item.description:
A senior software developer has thrown a medium-sized Agile delivery function into crisis after giving a daily stand-up update so clear, accurate and emotionally honest that several managers were forced to pretend they had not heard it.

Ryan Hollis, 52, delivered the update at 9:46am during what the company officially describes as a “collaborative delivery sync” and what employees privately describe as “the morning hostage roll call”.

When asked what he worked on yesterday, Hollis replied, “same shit as yesterday.”
